subsection_title: Designated forwarder election and split-brain conditions
text: In EVPN multihoming, each Ethernet Segment (ESI) elects a Designated Forwarder (DF) responsible for BUM traffic flooding toward the CE device. A split-brain condition occurs when the number of active links in an ESI drops below the expected count, causing the DF election to fail or produce an incorrect result. On Arista EOS, the DF election state is visible via 'show bgp evpn detail' and 'show evpn esi detail'. When active-links is less than expected-links, the DF may be None or pointing to a device that has lost connectivity. The recommended verification sequence is: (1) confirm ESI route type-1 advertisements from all PE devices, (2) check active link count on both PE devices, (3) verify DF election result matches the expected PE, (4) check for type-4 Ethernet Segment routes in the EVPN table. Safe read-only inspection should precede any configuration change.

section_title: EVPN Multihoming — ESI Split-Brain on Junos
subsection_title: DF election failure and recovery on QFX platforms
text: On Juniper QFX platforms, EVPN multihoming split-brain manifests when a physical interface forming part of an ESI goes down without the BGP session dropping. The ESI route type-1 withdrawal may be delayed, causing the remote PE to continue forwarding traffic toward the failed link. Junos verification commands include 'show evpn instance extensive', 'show evpn designated-forwarder', and 'show bgp neighbor' for the relevant EVPN peering. The DF election uses the lowest IP address algorithm by default. Recovery requires confirming both physical link state and BGP route withdrawal propagation. Do not clear EVPN state without first verifying the physical layer is restored.

section_title: EVPN Multihoming — ESI DF Election on SR Linux
subsection_title: Native path monitoring for ESI split-brain detection
text: Nokia SR Linux exposes ESI DF election state via native gNMI paths under /system/network-instance/protocols/evpn/ethernet-segments. The oper-designated-forwarder leaf indicates the current DF and updates in real time as link state changes. A split-brain is indicated when oper-designated-forwarder is null or mismatched between the two PE devices sharing the ESI. SR Linux supports both preference-based and lowest-IP DF election algorithms. When active-links drops to zero, the ESI is administratively withdrawn and all type-1 routes are retracted. Recovery is automatic once the physical link is restored and BGP reconverges — manual intervention is rarely required but the DF election state should be monitored to confirm convergence.
